description_long: This chapel is a stone structure with a symmetrical facade featuring two tall, pointed towers flanking a central entrance. The main building has arched windows and a steeply pitched roof, with a smaller annex on one side. The entrance is marked by three large arches supported by columns, leading into the main hall. Adjacent to the chapel is a freestanding bell tower with an open arch and a visible bell, as well as a small covered well or shrine. The exterior is finished in a muted stone texture, with dark roofing and minimal ornamentation. The surrounding area is a flat, open ground, possibly representing a courtyard or plaza.
description: A stone chapel with twin towers, arched windows, and a central entrance, accompanied by a bell tower and a small shrine in a courtyard setting.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with three arches, flanked by two tall towers and a visible bell tower to the right.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the length of the chapel, the pitched roof, and the annex, with the bell tower and shrine clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the chapel with the two towers, the bell tower to the left, and the shrine to the right.
description_view_3: The opposite side view highlights the annex and the side windows, with the bell tower and shrine partially visible behind the main structure.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: A freestanding bell tower is present to the left of the chapel, featuring an open arch and a visible bell at the top.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is set within a series of three large stone arches, likely made of wood, and appears to be dark in color with minimal decorative elements. The arches are supported by columns, giving the entrance a grand and formal appearance.
